scope.samplingNotes: Shell, tokens, and navigation audited in full. Page patterns sampled by structural type (list, detail, settings, workspace, admin). 273 shared components — sampled by directory (ui, shell, common, layout, flows, agchain, admin). Feature-specific one-offs (pipelines, documents, blocks, kv, agents, mcp) skipped unless they surfaced a foundation-level conflict.

shellOwnership.runtimeRole: Fixed top bar with search, project switcher, theme toggle. Shared across AppLayout and AgchainShellLayout.
shellOwnership.notes: Single implementation. Pages inject title/actions via HeaderCenterProvider context.
evidence: web/src/components/shell/TopCommandBar.tsx renders <header> with fixed position
web/src/components/shell/HeaderCenterContext.tsx provides useHeaderCenter() hook
TopCommandBar imported by AppLayout.tsx:~line 8 and AgchainShellLayout.tsx:~line 6

shellOwnership.runtimeRole: Main app sidebar navigation with collapsible/compact mode, project switcher, workspace selector
shellOwnership.notes: Single primary rail for /app/* routes under AppLayout. Other shell layouts use section-specific secondary rails instead.
evidence: web/src/components/shell/LeftRailShadcn.tsx renders <aside> with nav links
web/src/components/shell/nav-config.ts defines navigation structure and drill configs
LeftRailShadcn imported only by AppLayout.tsx

shellOwnership.runtimeRole: Section-specific secondary navigation rails for admin, AGChain, and flows detail surfaces
shellOwnership.notes: Five different secondary nav components with no shared base, contract, or width token. Each section builds its own rail from scratch. AdminLeftNav serves superuser + blockdata-admin + agchain-admin. AgchainLeftNav serves /app/agchain/*. FlowsDetailRail serves /app/flows/:id/*. Two AGChain sub-navs (Benchmark, Settings) add a third level.
evidence: web/src/components/admin/AdminLeftNav.tsx renders admin section links
web/src/components/agchain/AgchainLeftNav.tsx renders AGChain workspace links
web/src/components/flows/FlowsDetailRail.tsx renders flow detail tabs as rail links
web/src/components/agchain/AgchainBenchmarkNav.tsx and AgchainSettingsNav.tsx add sub-section navs
No shared SecondaryRail base component or width token found

shellOwnership.runtimeRole: Right panel container for help content and AI chat. Toggleable, with detachable chat window.
shellOwnership.notes: Single implementation with clear context provider. Persists open/closed and active tab to localStorage.
evidence: web/src/components/shell/RightRailShell.tsx renders <aside> for right panel
web/src/components/shell/RightRailContext.tsx manages open/close, active tab, chat detach state
web/src/components/shell/RightRailChatPanel.tsx and HomeHelpRail.tsx are content panels, not competing shells

shellOwnership.runtimeRole: Four authenticated shell layouts each frame <main> differently: AppLayout uses AppPageShell for constrained/fluid modes; AdminShellLayout uses fixed two-column; AgchainShellLayout mirrors AppLayout with AGChain workspace context; FlowsShellLayout uses full-height fixed sidebar + content.
shellOwnership.notes: AppLayout and AgchainShellLayout overlap significantly (both have header + left rail + optional right rail + content). AdminShellLayout and FlowsShellLayout serve genuinely different layouts. The overlap between AppLayout and AgchainShellLayout is the main ambiguity.
evidence: web/src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx wraps /app/* routes with TopCommandBar + LeftRailShadcn + RightRailShell
web/src/components/layout/AgchainShellLayout.tsx wraps /app/agchain/* with TopCommandBar + AgchainLeftNav + content
web/src/components/layout/AdminShellLayout.tsx wraps /app/superuser/*, /app/blockdata-admin/*, /app/agchain-admin/*
web/src/components/layout/FlowsShellLayout.tsx wraps /app/flows/:namespace/:flowId/*
web/src/components/layout/AppPageShell.tsx provides constrained/fluid content modes inside AppLayout

shellOwnership.runtimeRole: Three public-facing layouts: MarketingLayout for landing/integrations pages, PublicFullBleedLayout for login/register, PublicLayout for auth callbacks. All share PublicNav header.
shellOwnership.notes: Intentionally separate layouts for distinct public surfaces. No overlap or conflict — each serves a unique route group.
evidence: web/src/router.tsx assigns MarketingLayout to /, /integrations
web/src/router.tsx assigns PublicFullBleedLayout to /login, /register, /early-access
web/src/router.tsx assigns PublicLayout to /auth/callback, /auth/welcome

tokenInventory.driftNotes: Primary token source. 100+ CSS variables with complete light/dark via :root and :root[data-theme='light']. Also contains @theme inline mapping for Tailwind utility generation. Includes shell layout dimension tokens (header height, navbar width, content max-width).
evidence: web/src/tailwind.css:1-317 defines --background, --foreground, --card, --primary, --secondary, --muted, --border, --accent, --ring, --destructive and 50+ additional tokens
Dark mode defaults in :root, light overrides in :root[data-theme='light']

tokenInventory.driftNotes: 2,235 lines of AG Grid and react-data-grid (rdg) customization. Uses color-mix() utilities and references some CSS custom properties but also contains raw values. Grid-specific, not foundation-wide.
evidence: web/src/theme.css:1-2235 defines block viewer grid density, column alignment, cell styling, rdg styling

tokenInventory.driftNotes: Exports SURFACE_TOKENS, TEXT_TOKENS, BORDER_TOKENS, BRAND_TOKENS, ADMIN_CONFIG_TOKENS with hex pairs + cssVar references. Used by superadmin Design Standards page for live comparison. Should be the TypeScript accessor for CSS tokens, not an independent source.
evidence: web/src/lib/color-contract.ts defines day/night pairs with cssVar references like '--background'
Used by superadmin Design Standards page for live comparison

tokenInventory.driftNotes: FONT_FAMILIES, FONT_SIZES (2xs-4xl), FONT_WEIGHTS, FONT_RECIPES (11 usage recipes mapping contexts to Tailwind classes). Clean, well-structured.
evidence: web/src/lib/font-contract.ts exports FONT_FAMILIES, FONT_SIZES, FONT_WEIGHTS, FONT_STANDARD, FONT_RECIPES

tokenInventory.driftNotes: Two icon token files: icon-contract.ts (ICON_SIZES, ICON_CONTEXT_SIZE, ICON_STROKES, ICON_TONE_CLASS) and iconTokens.ts (defineIconToken factory for shell icons). icon-contract.ts notes Hugeicons migration in progress. iconTokens.ts may be superseded by migration.
evidence: web/src/lib/icon-contract.ts defines ICON_SIZES xs-xxl, ICON_STROKES, ICON_TONE_CLASS
web/src/lib/iconTokens.ts defines ICON_TOKENS with defineIconToken factory
icon-contract.ts ICON_STANDARD notes: Hugeicons migration in progress
web/src/lib/toolbar-contract.ts defines TOOLBAR_BUTTON, TOOLBAR_BUTTON_STATES, TOOLBAR_STRIP, TOOLBAR_BUTTON_BASE

tokenInventory.driftNotes: Runtime object with shell dimensions (header 60px/44px, navbar 220px/60px), grid light/dark color objects with raw hex, marketing tokens, admin nav width. Overlaps with CSS custom properties for shell dimensions. Grid colors duplicate what agGridTheme.ts consumes.
evidence: web/src/lib/styleTokens.ts exports shell.headerHeight (60px), shell.navbarWidth (220px), grid.dark/light objects with hex colors
Shell dimension tokens also defined as CSS custom properties in tailwind.css

tokenInventory.driftNotes: Maps 40+ shell tokens to Scalar API client tokens. Intentional bridge, not a competing source. Clean.
evidence: web/src/scalar-host/theme-contract.ts maps --primary to --scalar-color-accent, --background to --scalar-background-1, etc.

tokenInventory.source: Raw hex bypasses in UI primitives
tokenInventory.driftNotes: 7 Ark UI primitive wrappers use hardcoded hex: border-[#3a3a3a] (dark border), hover:bg-[#222221] (dark hover), outline-[#e2503f] (focus ring ~primary). These bypass --border, --accent, --primary tokens entirely. No light mode equivalents — dark-only hex values. This is the primary token system drift.
evidence: web/src/components/ui/accordion.tsx uses border-[#3a3a3a] and outline-[#e2503f]
web/src/components/ui/combobox.tsx uses border-[#3a3a3a], data-[highlighted]:bg-[#222221], data-[state=checked]:text-[#f47a5c]
web/src/components/ui/dialog.tsx uses border-[#3a3a3a], hover:bg-[#222221], outline-[#e2503f]
7 files total with same hex patterns

componentContracts.usageNotes: react-data-grid used for block viewer and settings sample. ag-grid used for marketing demo and theme factory. Two grid libraries in dependencies (ag-grid-react, react-data-grid).
evidence: web/src/components/blocks/BlockViewerGridRDG.tsx imports react-data-grid
web/src/components/marketing/MarketingGrid.tsx imports ag-grid-react
web/src/pages/settings/SettingsGridSample.tsx imports react-data-grid
web/src/lib/agGridTheme.ts creates AG Grid theme

componentContracts.usageNotes: AgchainEmptyState: semi-transparent card with title, description, optional action and eyebrow. FlowEmptyState: gradient icon container with title and subtitle. Different visual language for same role.
evidence: web/src/components/agchain/AgchainEmptyState.tsx used in AGChain pages
web/src/components/flows/tabs/FlowEmptyState.tsx used in flow tab views
No shared EmptyState in components/ui/ or components/common/

componentContracts.usageNotes: Single dismissible error alert with HugeIcons icon and destructive color. Used by DatabasePlaceholder and other pages for inline error display.
evidence: web/src/components/common/ErrorAlert.tsx renders dismissible error banner
Imported by web/src/pages/DatabasePlaceholder.tsx

componentContracts.usageNotes: Single Skeleton component with animate-pulse. Clean, no competing implementation.
evidence: web/src/components/ui/skeleton.tsx exports Skeleton with animate-pulse class

componentContracts.usageNotes: Shell-level page header used via HeaderCenterContext. Pages inject title and actions through the context provider, not by rendering the header directly.
evidence: web/src/components/shell/ShellPageHeader.tsx renders page-level header
web/src/components/shell/HeaderCenterContext.tsx provides injection hook

componentContracts.usageNotes: Single breadcrumb implementation. Usage scope not fully traced in sampling.
evidence: web/src/components/common/AppBreadcrumbs.tsx exists as sole breadcrumb component

pagePatterns.structureNotes: Projects.tsx: data table with pagination, loading, and toast feedback. Superuser list pages (FlowsList, plan tracker) use similar table pattern but with different table components.
pagePatterns.shellAlignmentNotes: Aligns with AppLayout + AppPageShell constrained mode.
evidence: web/src/pages/Projects.tsx uses pagination, loading states, toast
Superuser pages use table-based list patterns under AdminShellLayout

pagePatterns.structureNotes: FlowDetail uses FlowsShellLayout with fixed sidebar rail + main content with tabs. Superuser workspace editors use AdminShellLayout with a different rail pattern.
pagePatterns.shellAlignmentNotes: FlowDetail has its own shell layout. Superuser detail workspaces share AdminShellLayout.
evidence: web/src/components/layout/FlowsShellLayout.tsx wraps flow detail with FlowsDetailRail
web/src/pages/superuser/ detail pages use AdminShellLayout with PlanArtifactsRail or PlanUnitsRail

pagePatterns.structureNotes: SettingsLayout wraps /app/settings/* under AppLayout. AGChain has its own settings surface under AgchainShellLayout with a separate settings nav. Two settings architectures.
pagePatterns.shellAlignmentNotes: App settings nest inside AppLayout. AGChain settings nest inside AgchainShellLayout.
evidence: web/src/pages/settings/SettingsLayout.tsx wraps settings pages with Outlet
web/src/components/agchain/settings/AgchainSettingsNav.tsx provides AGChain-specific settings navigation

pagePatterns.structureNotes: Table + inspector pane pattern used in superuser surfaces. AdminShellLayout provides the two-column frame. Inspector panes appear as rails.
evidence: web/src/pages/superuser/PlanMetadataPane.tsx renders detail inspector
web/src/pages/superuser/PlanArtifactsRail.tsx and PlanUnitsRail.tsx serve as inspection rails

pagePatterns.structureNotes: 40 AGChain page files under AgchainShellLayout with AgchainWorkspaceProvider context. Workspace-scoped with organization/project selection. Distinct surface with its own nav, settings, and empty states.
pagePatterns.shellAlignmentNotes: Self-contained under AgchainShellLayout. Does not use AppLayout patterns.
evidence: web/src/pages/agchain/ contains 40 page-level files
web/src/contexts/AgchainWorkspaceContext.tsx manages workspace scope

pagePatterns.structureNotes: Multi-panel explorer with table catalog sidebar, column metadata, row grid. Handles catalog loading, row loading, empty, and error states individually.
evidence: web/src/pages/DatabasePlaceholder.tsx manages catalogError, rowsError, loadingCatalog, loadingRows states

navigationStructure.routeMapping: web/src/router.tsx defines the full route tree with nested layout wrappers. Routes group under MarketingLayout, PublicFullBleedLayout, PublicLayout (public), and AuthGuard → AppLayout, AdminShellLayout, AgchainShellLayout, FlowsShellLayout (authenticated).
navigationStructure.breadcrumbConventions: AppBreadcrumbs component exists but usage scope not fully traced. Pages primarily use ShellPageHeader via HeaderCenterContext for page identity.
navigationStructure.actionPlacement: Primary actions placed in page headers via HeaderCenterContext right slot. Table row actions use dropdown menus. Toolbar actions use toolbar-contract.ts patterns.
evidence: web/src/router.tsx groups routes under layout components with nested Outlet rendering
web/src/components/shell/LeftRailShadcn.tsx is the sole primary rail
web/src/components/shell/nav-config.ts defines navigation items and drill configs
5 different secondary navigation components with no shared base

statePresentation.loading: Mixed. AuthGuard and SuperuserGuard use inline spinners/text ('Verifying access...'). AgchainProjectPlaceholderPage uses 'Loading AGChain project...' text. DatabasePlaceholder tracks loadingCatalog and loadingRows separately. Skeleton component exists but is not the universal pattern.
statePresentation.empty: Fragmented. AgchainEmptyState (card with title/description/action) and FlowEmptyState (gradient icon + title/subtitle) serve the same role with different visual language. DatabasePlaceholder has inline 'No tables match...' and 'No rows...' text. No shared foundation EmptyState component.
statePresentation.error: ErrorAlert component exists in components/common/ but is not universally used. AGChain placeholder pages have their own error-with-retry pattern. SuperuserGuard has inline error text with retry. No ErrorBoundary at shell level detected.
statePresentation.success: Consistent. Sonner toast library via UIProvider positioned top-right with rich colors. toast() imported from 'sonner' across pages.
statePresentation.permission: Well-structured. AuthGuard redirects unauthenticated to /login. SuperuserGuard handles three admin surface tiers (superuser, blockdata-admin, agchain-admin) with AdminSurfaceGuard. Auth bypass available via VITE_AUTH_BYPASS in development.
statePresentation.async: No shared long-running/polling pattern identified. Suspense used in one place (useWorkspaceEditor for lazy-loaded editors).
evidence: web/src/auth/AuthGuard.tsx shows spinner during session check, redirects to /login
web/src/pages/superuser/SuperuserGuard.tsx shows 'Verifying access...' with retry
web/src/components/agchain/AgchainEmptyState.tsx and web/src/components/flows/tabs/FlowEmptyState.tsx are two competing empty state components
web/src/components/common/ErrorAlert.tsx is the only shared error component
web/src/components/ui/provider.tsx renders Toaster from sonner at top-right
web/src/components/ui/skeleton.tsx exports Skeleton with animate-pulse

accessibilityNotes: Focus rings present on interactive elements via Tailwind ring utilities and outline-[#e2503f] in UI primitives, but the focus ring color is hardcoded hex rather than using --ring token.
Three icon libraries with different size conventions may produce inconsistent touch targets.
No ARIA landmarks audited at shell level beyond semantic HTML elements (<header>, <nav>, <main>, <aside>).
Dark mode uses :root[data-theme='light'] override pattern — contrast ratios for muted text not verified.
UI primitive hex bypasses (#3a3a3a borders) are dark-mode-only values with no light mode equivalent, which would break if the component is rendered in light mode with the hardcoded border.

conflictBundles.roleUnderDispute: Canonical icon library and icon component contract

competingImplementations.name: Tabler Icons
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Legacy/primary icon library with widest current usage across the codebase

competingImplementations.name: Hugeicons
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Migration target per icon-contract.ts. Richer icon set with stroke weight control. Used in newer components.

competingImplementations.name: Lucide React
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Used in UI primitives (likely from shadcn/ui scaffolding). Lightweight, consistent with shadcn conventions.

evidence: grep shows @tabler/icons-react imported in 101 files
grep shows @hugeicons/react imported in 27 files
grep shows lucide-react imported in 8 files
web/src/lib/icon-contract.ts ICON_STANDARD notes 'Hugeicons migration in progress'
web-docs/src/content/docs/internal/style-guide/icon-unification.md documents full migration plan
conflictBundles.whyNoSingleContract: Three icon libraries accumulated over time. Tabler was the original choice. Hugeicons was selected as the migration target for its richer set and stroke control. Lucide came in via shadcn/ui primitives. Migration plan exists in docs but Phase 1 has not been executed. All three coexist in production.
conflictBundles.recommendedDirection: Execute the documented Hugeicons migration plan. Hugeicons is already the declared target with an AppIcon abstraction planned. Phase 1 (foundation: AppIcon redesign, nav config, UI primitives, shell) should precede Phase 2 (systematic file-by-file migration). Decision rule 2 (broadest legitimate reuse) favors Tabler short-term but rule 6 (easiest to enforce) favors Hugeicons with the AppIcon contract.
discussionQuestions: Is the Hugeicons migration still the intended direction, or has the team reconsidered?
Should the AppIcon abstraction be built first (gating all future icon usage) or should migration proceed file-by-file?
What is the timeline priority for icon migration vs other foundation work?

conflictBundles.roleUnderDispute: Token system compliance in Ark UI wrapper components

competingImplementations.name: CSS custom property tokens
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Semantic border, accent, and primary tokens with full light/dark coverage (--border, --accent, --primary, --ring)

competingImplementations.name: Hardcoded hex values in UI primitives
competingImplementations.location: web/src/components/ui/accordion.tsx, checkbox.tsx, collapsible.tsx, combobox.tsx, dialog.tsx, file-upload.tsx, menu.tsx
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Quick dark-mode-specific styling for Ark UI component wrappers. Uses #3a3a3a (border), #222221 (hover bg), #e2503f (focus ring), #f47a5c (checked state).

evidence: web/src/components/ui/accordion.tsx uses border-[#3a3a3a] and outline-[#e2503f]
web/src/components/ui/combobox.tsx uses border-[#3a3a3a], bg-[#222221], text-[#f47a5c]
web/src/components/ui/dialog.tsx uses border-[#3a3a3a], bg-[#222221], outline-[#e2503f]
web/src/tailwind.css defines --border, --ring, --accent, --primary as semantic tokens
Hex values have no light-mode equivalents — dark-only
conflictBundles.whyNoSingleContract: The Ark UI primitives were likely scaffolded with inline hex values before the token system was mature, or were copied from an external source without token adaptation. The values approximate the semantic tokens but do not reference them, creating a parallel dark-mode-only style layer.
conflictBundles.recommendedDirection: Replace all hardcoded hex values in the 7 UI primitive files with the corresponding CSS custom property references: border-[#3a3a3a] → border-border, hover:bg-[#222221] → hover:bg-accent, outline-[#e2503f] → outline-ring (or outline-primary). This is a low-risk, high-impact cleanup that immediately restores light mode correctness and token system authority. Decision rule 1 (foundation-owned) and rule 4 (light+dark coherence).
discussionQuestions: Are these hex values intentional dark-mode-specific overrides, or accidental drift from the token system?
Should the cleanup be done as a batch (all 7 files at once) or file-by-file with visual verification?
Are there other Ark UI primitives beyond these 7 that also bypass tokens?

conflictBundles.roleUnderDispute: Canonical data grid component for tabular data display

competingImplementations.name: react-data-grid
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Lightweight data grid for block viewer and settings sample. MIT-licensed, smaller bundle.

competingImplementations.name: ag-grid-react
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Feature-rich commercial grid for marketing demo page. Has dedicated theme factory.

evidence: web/src/components/blocks/BlockViewerGridRDG.tsx imports react-data-grid
web/src/pages/settings/SettingsGridSample.tsx imports react-data-grid
web/src/components/marketing/MarketingGrid.tsx imports ag-grid-react
web/src/lib/agGridTheme.ts creates AG Grid theme via themeQuartz.withParams()
Both ag-grid-react and react-data-grid listed in web/package.json dependencies
conflictBundles.whyNoSingleContract: Two grid libraries serve different surfaces (marketing vs app). Neither was chosen as the canonical grid. AG Grid has a theme factory but is only used in marketing. react-data-grid is used in the actual app surfaces but has no theme factory.
conflictBundles.recommendedDirection: Standardize on react-data-grid for app surfaces. ag-grid is only used in the marketing demo — evaluate whether the marketing page can be rebuilt with react-data-grid or whether ag-grid should remain as a marketing-only dependency. Decision rule 2 (broadest reuse) favors react-data-grid for app code.
discussionQuestions: Is ag-grid needed for any planned app features, or is it marketing-only?
Can the marketing grid demo be rebuilt with react-data-grid to eliminate the ag-grid dependency?
Should react-data-grid get a theme factory similar to agGridTheme.ts?

competingImplementations.name: AdminLeftNav
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Left nav for superuser, blockdata-admin, and agchain-admin surfaces under AdminShellLayout

competingImplementations.name: AgchainLeftNav
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Left nav for AGChain workspace under AgchainShellLayout

competingImplementations.name: FlowsDetailRail
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Fixed sidebar rail for flow detail view under FlowsShellLayout

competingImplementations.name: AgchainBenchmarkNav + AgchainSettingsNav
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Sub-section navigation within AGChain for benchmark and settings views

evidence: web/src/components/admin/AdminLeftNav.tsx renders admin section links
web/src/components/agchain/AgchainLeftNav.tsx renders AGChain workspace links
web/src/components/flows/FlowsDetailRail.tsx renders flow detail tabs as rail links
web/src/components/agchain/AgchainBenchmarkNav.tsx and AgchainSettingsNav.tsx add AGChain sub-navs
No shared SecondaryRail, SectionNav, or DetailRail base component found
conflictBundles.whyNoSingleContract: Each section built its own secondary rail independently. Admin, AGChain, and Flows all need a left rail with section links, but no shared primitive was extracted. Width, padding, active-state styling, and link rendering differ across implementations.
conflictBundles.recommendedDirection: Extract a shared SecondaryRail or SectionNav primitive that accepts a link config array and renders consistently. AdminLeftNav is the broadest current user (serves 3 admin surfaces) and should be the structural reference. Section-specific content (AGChain workspace scope, flow detail tabs) can be injected via render props or slots. Decision rule 2 (broadest reuse) and rule 7 (clearest ownership).
discussionQuestions: Should all secondary rails share a single width token, or should different sections be allowed different widths?
Should the secondary rail support collapse behavior like the primary LeftRailShadcn?
Should the AGChain sub-navs (BenchmarkNav, SettingsNav) be expressed as nested sections within AgchainLeftNav rather than separate components?

conflictBundles.roleUnderDispute: Reusable empty-state component for pages and tab views

competingImplementations.name: AgchainEmptyState
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Semi-transparent card with title, description, optional action button and eyebrow label

competingImplementations.name: FlowEmptyState
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Gradient background icon container with title and optional subtitle

evidence: web/src/components/agchain/AgchainEmptyState.tsx renders card-style empty state
web/src/components/flows/tabs/FlowEmptyState.tsx renders gradient-icon empty state
No shared EmptyState component in web/src/components/ui/ or web/src/components/common/
web/src/pages/DatabasePlaceholder.tsx uses inline text for empty states, not either component
conflictBundles.whyNoSingleContract: AGChain and Flows built their own empty states independently with different visual language. Neither lives in a shared location. DatabasePlaceholder uses neither and has inline empty text. No foundation-level empty state contract was established.
conflictBundles.recommendedDirection: Create a shared EmptyState component in components/common/ or components/ui/ that supports the AgchainEmptyState's feature set (title, description, action, eyebrow) as it covers the broader API. FlowEmptyState's gradient icon treatment can be a variant prop. Decision rule 1 (foundation-owned) and rule 3 (fewest local overrides).
discussionQuestions: Should the canonical empty state use the card style (AgchainEmptyState) or the gradient icon style (FlowEmptyState)?
Should it support both as variants, or should one visual language be chosen?
Should inline empty text (like DatabasePlaceholder's 'No rows...') also use the component, or are simple text empties acceptable?

conflictBundles.roleUnderDispute: Authenticated shell layout for header + left-rail + content surfaces

competingImplementations.name: AppLayout
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Main application shell with TopCommandBar, LeftRailShadcn, RightRailShell, HeaderCenterProvider, and RightRailProvider

competingImplementations.name: AgchainShellLayout
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: AGChain workspace shell with TopCommandBar, AgchainLeftNav, HeaderCenterProvider, and AgchainWorkspaceProvider — structurally mirrors AppLayout but with different left rail and workspace context

evidence: web/src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx renders TopCommandBar + LeftRailShadcn + RightRailShell + Outlet
web/src/components/layout/AgchainShellLayout.tsx renders TopCommandBar + AgchainLeftNav + Outlet with resizable sidebar
Both use HeaderCenterProvider for page title injection
Both use TopCommandBar as the header
Both implement resizable left sidebar with localStorage persistence
AppLayout has right rail; AgchainShellLayout does not
conflictBundles.whyNoSingleContract: AgchainShellLayout was built as a separate layout to accommodate AGChain's workspace context and different left rail. However, its structural shell (header + resizable sidebar + content) is nearly identical to AppLayout. The two diverged because AGChain needed AgchainWorkspaceProvider at the layout level, but the shell framing could have been shared.
conflictBundles.recommendedDirection: Evaluate whether AgchainShellLayout can be expressed as AppLayout with a different left-rail slot and an additional context provider. If AppLayout supported slot injection for the left rail, AGChain could use it directly with AgchainLeftNav + AgchainWorkspaceProvider. This would reduce shell duplication and ensure shell behavior (resize, persistence, header) stays in sync. Decision rule 5 (composes cleanly with shell model) and rule 6 (easiest to enforce).
discussionQuestions: Should AppLayout become a configurable shell that accepts left-rail and context-provider slots?
Does AGChain need its own shell long-term, or should it converge with the main app shell?
Should the right rail (help/AI) be available in AGChain workspace, or is its absence intentional?

cleanAreas.summary: TopCommandBar is the single header component shared across AppLayout and AgchainShellLayout. HeaderCenterContext provides a clean injection mechanism for page-specific content. No competing header implementations.
evidence: web/src/components/shell/TopCommandBar.tsx is the sole header component
web/src/components/shell/HeaderCenterContext.tsx provides useHeaderCenter() hook
TopCommandBar imported by AppLayout.tsx and AgchainShellLayout.tsx

cleanAreas.summary: RightRailShell + RightRailContext provide a single-owner right panel with help and AI chat tabs. State persists to localStorage. AssistantDockHost handles detached chat window. No competing implementations.
evidence: web/src/components/shell/RightRailShell.tsx renders the right panel
web/src/components/shell/RightRailContext.tsx manages panel state
web/src/components/shell/RightRailChatPanel.tsx and HomeHelpRail.tsx are content, not competing shells

cleanAreas.summary: AuthGuard is the single authentication wrapper. Redirects unauthenticated users to /login. Supports VITE_AUTH_BYPASS for development. Clean, no competing auth patterns.
evidence: web/src/auth/AuthGuard.tsx wraps all /app/* routes in router.tsx
web/src/auth/AuthContext.tsx provides useAuth() hook

cleanAreas.summary: Sonner toast library is the single notification mechanism. UIProvider renders Toaster at top-right with rich colors. toast() imported consistently from 'sonner' across pages.
evidence: web/src/components/ui/provider.tsx renders <Toaster> from sonner
toast() imported from 'sonner' in Projects.tsx, BlockViewerGridRDG.tsx, and other pages

cleanAreas.summary: Single useTheme hook with data-theme attribute on :root. Theme persists to localStorage. Hydrated in main.tsx before React render to prevent flash. No competing theme mechanisms.
evidence: web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts provides useTheme() hook
web/src/main.tsx hydrates theme from localStorage before mount
web/src/tailwind.css uses :root and :root[data-theme='light'] for token overrides

cleanAreas.summary: tailwind.css is the single source of truth for CSS custom properties. 100+ semantic tokens with full light/dark coverage via :root and :root[data-theme='light']. @theme inline mapping generates Tailwind utilities. No competing CSS token source.
evidence: web/src/tailwind.css defines all semantic CSS custom properties
TypeScript contracts (color-contract.ts, font-contract.ts) reference CSS vars, not replace them
No separate tokens.css or theme-variables.css competing

cleanAreas.summary: SuperuserGuard handles three admin surface tiers (superuser, blockdata-admin, agchain-admin) via a shared AdminSurfaceGuard pattern. Clean three-tier access model with consistent loading, error, and redirect behavior.
evidence: web/src/pages/superuser/SuperuserGuard.tsx exports SuperuserGuard, BlockdataAdminGuard, AgchainAdminGuard
All three use AdminSurfaceGuard internally with surface-specific access checks

cleanAreas.summary: font-contract.ts provides a single, well-structured typography contract with FONT_FAMILIES, FONT_SIZES, FONT_WEIGHTS, and 11 FONT_RECIPES mapping contexts to Tailwind classes. No competing typography definitions.
evidence: web/src/lib/font-contract.ts exports complete typography contract
FONT_RECIPES map page-title, section-heading, body-text, nav, badges, code-cells to exact Tailwind classes

cleanAreas.summary: Three public layouts (MarketingLayout, PublicFullBleedLayout, PublicLayout) serve distinct route groups with no overlap. PublicNav shared header. Intentionally separate, not competing.
evidence: web/src/router.tsx assigns each public layout to specific route groups
web/src/components/layout/PublicNav.tsx is the shared public header
